title: ferray-fft: no negative axis support — NumPy allows axis=-1
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's FFT functions accept negative axis indices: np.fft.fft(a, axis=-1) means the last axis, axis=-2 means second-to-last. ferray-fft's resolve_axis() only accepts usize (non-negative). Passing a negative axis is a type error at compile time. While the default (None -> last axis) covers the most common case, explicit negative indexing is common in NumPy code. Either accept isize for axis parameters and normalize, or document the difference.
